model.label: MiMo-V2-Flash
model.description: MiMo • Flash-tier model configured through a custom endpoint for fast inference and lightweight agent tasks.

pick.rationale: Alabama holds a slight edge in offensive firepower, ranking 3rd nationally in adjusted offensive efficiency, which should challenge Texas Tech's 27th-ranked defense. While Texas Tech boasts a stronger overall defensive rating and a more balanced efficiency profile, Alabama's experience in the SEC and proven ability to score in bunches provide a credible path to victory. The Red Raiders' upset potential relies on controlling tempo and limiting Alabama's transition opportunities, but the Crimson Tide's elite offense and recent form make them the more reliable pick to advance.

reasoningStep.title: Team Quality and Efficiency Analysis
reasoningStep.summary: Comparing raw ratings from KenPom and Torvik to assess overall team strength and matchup dynamics.
reasoningStep.evidence: Texas Tech ranks 12th in Torvik and 19th in KenPom with a top-10 offense (9th in Torvik OE) and 27th-ranked defense (Torvik DE). Alabama ranks 17th in Torvik and 16th in KenPom with the nation's 3rd-ranked offense (Torvik OE) but a 67th-ranked defense (Torvik DE). The efficiency gap is narrow, with Texas Tech holding a slight defensive edge and Alabama a significant offensive advantage.

result.snippet: The 2025-26 Texas Tech Red Raiders basketball team represents Texas Tech University during the 2025-26 NCAA Division I men's basketball season as a member of the Big 12 Conference. The Red Raiders are led by third-year coach Grant McCasland, and play their home games at the United Supermarkets Arena located in Lubbock, Texas .

result.title: Texas Tech to Face Akron in NCAA Tournament Round One
result.snippet: The Texas Tech men's basketball team has earned a spot in the 2026 NCAA Tournament, drawing the #5 seed in the Midwest Region. They will face off against #12 seed Akron in the first round on Friday in Tampa, Florida. Texas Tech is making its 22nd NCAA Tournament appearance, led by head coach Grant McCasland, who emphasized the team's focus on preparing for the dangerous 5-12 matchup.

modelTrace.content:
You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.
